source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple sessions in same cwd
notes: Two concurrent opencode headless `opencode run` chains in the SAME cwd, driven via the driver's start_session step + inline {"session":N} targeting (no tmux/TUI — start_session stays on the headless path, only reset_session/slash force run_live). opencode keys sessions on the SQLite session.id; the directory column is only a liveness gate (watcher.go scanSessions / liveCWDs), so two same-cwd chains are two independent ses_… rows with independent working→ready arcs — daemon_capability: full. The spec anchors the FIRST phase on the first WORKING transition rather than the first ready, because the shared process scanner emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready that never enters working; anchoring on ready would greedily match that placeholder and cascade same_session_as failures (transport-neutral pitfall — applies to opencode's ProcessOwnedStore too). Windows are wide (≤60s) because the dev default is a slow local LM Studio model (~30-45s/turn). Assertions: (1) session 1 does an independent working→ready turn; (2) a DISTINCT ses_… appears (s2_birth carries new_session:true) and does its own working→ready turn; (3) session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is alive (s1_turn2_*, pinned same_session_as s1_turn1_start), proving the per-session binding stayed stable and never flapped/merged across the two same-cwd rows.

text: Session 1 first prompt drives ready → working (anchors the real ses_… session; the pre-session proc-<PID> row never enters working)
Session 1 first turn completes: working → ready
Session 2 starts in the SAME cwd as a DISTINCT ses_… and appears in ready (new_session proves it is not session 1 — two independent SQLite session rows, not one)
Session 2 first prompt drives ready → working — independent of session 1
Session 2 first turn completes: working → ready
Session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is still alive — a working transition on session 1's original ses_… proves the binding stayed stable and did not flap/merge into session 2
Session 1 second turn completes: working → ready
